|death=By [[2 BBY/Legends|2 BBY]]<ref name="Darklighter">According to the entry for [[Soontir Fel]] in ''[[The New Essential Guide to Characters]]'', this event occurs two years after the [[Battle of Nar Shaddaa (4 BBY)|Battle of Nar Shaddaa]]. However, later sources shifted the battle’s date to [[4 BBY/Legends|4 BBY]]. Works published around the same time as Characters, such as ''[[The New Essential Chronology]]'', instead placed the battle in [[3 BBY/Legends|3 BBY]], which aligns with the timeline presented in [[Empire 8|''Star Wars: Empire'' issue 8]], depicting the mutiny shortly before the [[Battle of Yavin/Legends|Battle of Yavin]]. More recent references, including ''[[The Essential Reader's Companion]]'', ultimately standardized the Battle of Nar Shaddaa as occurring in 4 BBY.</ref>

{{Quote|Masla!|[[General/Legends|General]] [[Roons Sewell]]'s battle cry|<ref name="Star Wars: Empire: The Short, Happy Life of Roons Sewell">''[[Star Wars: Empire: The Short, Happy Life of Roons Sewell]]''</ref>}}
'''Masla''' was a [[Human/Legends|Human]] [[Gender/Legends|female]] [[Actor/Legends|actress]] in the same drama group as [[Roons Sewell]].

==Biography==
[[File:IThinkWeShouldSeeOtherPeople-E10.jpg|thumb|upright|left|Sewell and the body of Masla.]]
Masla eventually had a romance with the future [[General/Legends|general]] of the [[Alliance to Restore the Republic/Legends|Rebel Alliance]]. But this romance ended when the [[Galactic Empire/Legends|Imperials]] burst onto the stage, accusing the actors of being promoters of a subversive, inflammatory and seditious play. However, Sewell refused to stop performing. The [[Stormtrooper/Legends|stormtroopers]] opened fire against the actors, and Roons ran to protect Masla, but it was too late. Masla received a lethal [[Blaster/Legends|blaster]] shot.

In battle, Sewell would yell her name out whenever he fired at an Imperial.

==Appearances==
*[[Empire 10|''Empire'' 10]] {{1st}} {{Flash}}
*[[Empire 11|''Empire'' 11]] {{Flash}}
